MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-26-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Michael E. Sandgren of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Benjamin Moore & Co.
J. Bruce Jackson of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (510-844-5500), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Sharon Dunn, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT BENJAMIN MOORE & COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / SUMMARY ADJUDICATION

Both sides submitted to the Court's tentative. Tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Benjamin Moore & Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages is granted. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Charles Dunn, Jr.'s deposition testimony precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar, as it shows that plaintiffs have evidence that decedent performed acts (sanding) that exposed him to Kuhls Seamlast, the only argument made by defendant in its summary judgment motion. However, even if defendant had made asbestos content of such product an issue, Charles Dunn, Jr.'s deposition testimony, defendant's General Order No. 129 interrogatory responses and Mr. Minchew's deposition testimony create a triable issue whether decedent was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' claim for punitive damages did not violate CRC 3.1350 and plaintiffs' objections thereto are overruled. Defendant sustained its initial burden and plaintiffs failed to present clear and convincing evidence that defendant acted with the requisite malice, fraud or oppression to warrant the imposition of punitive damages. Plaintiffs' objection regarding defendant attaching one verification to plaintiffs' special interrogatory responses is overruled. Defendant's objections to Charles Dunn, Jr.'s deposition testimony are overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported (503/TLJ)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication;
Off Calendar Feb-06-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Stephen M. Fishback of KELLER, FISHBACK & JACKSON LLP (818-342-7442), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Sharon Dunn, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT MERLEX STUCCO, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION;

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Merlex Stucco, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication are denied. As to summary judgment and summary adjudication issues 1, 2, 5 and 6, defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Charles Dunn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Charles Dunn, Jr.'s deposition testimony and defendant's failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention that Charles Dunn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant failed to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Even if defendant had sustained its initial burden, the deposition testimony of Charles Dunn, Jr. and William Ruth, defendant's responses to standard interrogatories and Eugene Carpenter's declaration would create a triable issue whether Charles Dunn was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials (Merlex stucco) attributable to defendant. As to summary adjudication of issues 3, 4 and 7, defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence supporting their false representation and intentional tort causes of action and claim for punitive damages. Defendant's failure to demonstrate that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support these claims precludes defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Plaintiffs' objections to Susan Verburg's deposition (Exhibit O) are sustained. All plaintiffs' other objections are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Carpenter's declaration would be overruled if the court reached plaintiffs' evidence. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Tamara Willat of WILLAT COURT REPORTING, CSR #4609 (Tel: 415-272-1753) (503/TLJ)
